channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Implementation of a prospective video databank for artificial intelligence model development, validation and clinical outcomes research in gastrointestinal endoscopy.
item.description: Gastrointestinal endoscopy - High-quality, annotated datasets are fundamental to clinical research and artificial intelligence (AI) model development. Existing endoscopic databanks are predominantly retrospective, image-based,...

item.title: Gastrointestinal endoscopic image classification using a hybrid modified inception network and a customized vision transformer with tree growth feature selection.
item.description: Scientific reports - The Gastrointestinal (GI) tract plays a vital role in digestion by breaking down food into essential nutrients. Disorders such as bleeding lesions, ulcerative colitis, Inflammatory Bowel Disease (I...

item.title: Telemonitoring in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Findings from the TIGE-Rus Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: Telemedicine is increasingly used in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but its effects on quality of life (QoL) and psychological outcomes remain unclear. Objectives: This study aimed t...

item.title: EPHX2 Orchestrates Intestinal Epithelial Barrier Repair in Ulcerative Colitis: An Integrated Multi-Omics and Experimental Study.
item.description: Clinical and translational science - Genetic determinants are central to ulcerative colitis (UC) pathogenesis, yet their identification via single-cell transcriptomics and clinical translation remains incomplete. We integrated GWAS-eQ...

item.title: Coal Dust-Mimetic Carbon Nanoparticles Induce Colonic Epithelial Cell Senescence via p16/p21 Dual Pathways and Promote Colonic Inflammatory Response.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease among coal miners is globally higher than that in the general population; however, the underlying pathogenic mechanisms remain unclear. Given that the co...

item.title: Mapping the evolutionary landscape of intestinal organoids: from self-organization biology to precision medicine.
item.description: Stem cell research & therapy - Review - The advent of intestinal organoids has fundamentally transformed biomedical research by providing a physiological ex vivo model that recapitulates native tissue architecture. Despite an exponential...

item.title: HSP27 in autoimmune diseases: Context-dependent regulation and clinical potential.
item.description: Biochimica et biophysica acta. Molecular basis of disease - Review - Heat shock protein 27 (HSP27) is a crucial member of the small heat shock protein family with a molecular weight of approximately 27 kDa, playing key roles in cellular stress response, protein home...

item.title: Thalidomide for CGD-related inflammatory bowel disease: A randomized, double-blind trial.
item.description: Journal of human immunity - Chronic granulomatous disease-related inflammatory bowel disease (CGD-IBD) requires effective and safe therapies, as conventional immunosuppressants increase infection risk. Thalidomide, with anti-...

item.title: Adverse Events Associated with Advanced Therapy Compared with Conventional Therapy in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Common Data Model Analysis.
item.description: Gut and liver - Although clinical trials have reported adverse events (AEs) associated with advanced therapies (ATs) in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), the risks of AEs may be underestimated due to...

item.title: Paeoniae Decoction attenuates chronic colitis-associated intestinal fibrosis by suppressing TGF-β/Smad-mediated epithelial-mesenchymal transition and remodeling the metabolic microenvironment.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Paeoniae Decoction (PD) is a classic Chinese medicine formula for treating intestinal diseases. However, its direct intervention effect on intestinal fibrosis and the underlying molecular mechanism...

item.title: Proximally dominant inflammation at colonoscopy predicts pan-colonic histological remission following oral vancomycin therapy in pediatric ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Digestive and liver disease : official journal of the Italian Society of Gastroenterology and the Italian Association for the Study of the Liver - Oral vancomycin therapy (OVT) induces histological remission in children with ulcerative colitis (UC) showing primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC)-associated features (proximally dominant or patchy...

item.title: Complete clinical and endoscopic remission in severe ulcerative pancolitis following personalized Ayurveda management: A case report.
item.description: Explore (New York, N.Y.) - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic, idiopathic inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) characterised by relapsing-remitting mucosal inflammation commencing in the rectum and extending proximally towards...

item.title: Basal plasmacytosis and eosinophilia for distinguishing inflammatory bowel disease from gastrointestinal tuberculosis on mucosal biopsy.
item.description: Journal of clinical pathology - Accurate distinction of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and gastrointestinal tuberculosis (GITB) on mucosal biopsies remains challenging, especially in South-East Asia. Our previous meta-analysis ...

item.title: Respiratory syncytial virus incidence and clinical outcomes in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a National Veterans Affairs cohort study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Data describing the incidence and clinical outcomes of respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infection among patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are limited. We conducted a nationwide retros...

item.title: Dietary B vitamin intake and risk of ulcerative colitis: a case-control study.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - A meta-analysis showed that serum folate concentrations, but not vitamin B12 concentrations, are lower in patients with ulcerative colitis compared to healthy controls. However, no epidemiological ...

item.title: Uveitis in spondyloarthritis: clinical patterns and differences between axial and peripheral forms.
item.description: Clinical rheumatology - The association between uveitis and spondyloarthritis is well established. However, its characterization across the different types of spondyloarthritis remains limited in the literature. Therefore...

item.title: Pyoderma Gangrenosum: Association of Ulcer Size With Clinical Characteristics and Healing Outcomes.
item.description: The Australasian journal of dermatology - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is an ulcerative inflammatory dermatosis that is poorly characterised from a diagnostic and therapeutic perspective. We evaluated the clinical characteristics and healing ...

item.title: The at-risk-to-early continuum of enteropathic spondyloarthritis.
item.description: The Lancet. Rheumatology - Review - Enteropathic spondyloarthritis is a common, under-recognised musculoskeletal manifestation of inflammatory bowel disease and a potentially preventable contributor to long-term disability. Epidemiol...

item.title: Global trends in the burden of inflammatory bowel disease from 1990 to 2021: socioeconomic and sex-specific disparities with exploratory mendelian randomization analyses.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) shows substantial variation in burden across regions and populations worldwide. However, patterns of IBD b3urden across levels of socio-demographic development rema...

item.title: Diverse clinical spectrum of Niemann-Pick C: insights from a single center.
item.description: Journal of pediatric endocrinology & metabolism : JPEM - Niemann-Pick disease type C (NPC) is a lysosomal lipid trafficking disorder characterized by impaired intracellular cholesterol transport, resulting in progressive neurovisceral involvement. While ...
